trace_target.label: invariant sync-gate runtime tooling (reader + walker + drift detector)

requirements.title: Clean-exit contract — pnpm keel-invariants:check exits 0 when manifest ↔ anchors ↔ hashes align; non-zero with structured drift report otherwise
requirements.substrate_verification: Task 5 clean-path smoke: `pnpm keel-invariants:check` → exit 0, no stderr (story file lines 94, 139). AC 1 + AC 7 end-to-end at shell-invocation. Not a test-runner assertion; evidence captured at dev-story.
requirements.scope_notes: Primary gate-clean happy path. sync-gate.ts#runSyncGate dedupes sourcePath reads via `uniqueSourcePaths` set + `Promise.all`; hash compare bails early on mismatch. check.ts resolves repoRoot via import.meta.dirname + path.resolve back to repo root.

requirements.title: Addition drift — new rule in source without manifest entry → reports added-to-source-only, exits non-zero
requirements.substrate_verification: Schema-level via InvariantsSchema.superRefine id-uniqueness refine (Story 1.9 Task 3, story file line 75). Anchor-walker branch realises anchor-side half of addition drift (INVARIANTS.md anchor without manifest row → `removed-from-docs-only`). Structural (not exercised in smoke — substrate has 0 live addition drift).
requirements.scope_notes: Story 1.9 scope carve-out (AC 2 lines 23, 128): source-tree auto-discovery of unregistered rules is DEFERRED — anchor-side realisation only at 1.9. The 10 invariants are intentionally registered; authors of future rules MUST add anchor + manifest row in the same PR (contract).
requirements.level_notes: schema + structural; adversarial CR pass is agreed backstop

requirements.title: Removal drift (source side) — manifest entry whose sourcePath is deleted → reports removed-from-source-only, exits non-zero
requirements.substrate_verification: Structural only — sync-gate.ts path: `fs.readFile` rejection → `actualHash = null` → Drift kind `removed-from-source-only` (story file line 141 branch). Not exercised in smoke (all 8 distinct sourcePath files exist on baseline).
requirements.scope_notes: Small pure-function branch; TypeScript compile-time check + adversarial CR pass are the agreed verification surface at 1.9. Unit-test landing in Story 1.16 (test-runner wiring) can backfill.

requirements.title: Edit drift — source edit without manifest contentHash bump → reports content-hash-mismatch, exits non-zero
requirements.substrate_verification: Task 5 drift-path smoke: mutated `packages/keel-invariants/tsconfig.base.json` (1-char append) → exit 1 + structured JSON DriftReport on stderr for `INV-tsconfig-base` as `content-hash-mismatch` with expected + actual hashes (story file line 95, 142). Revert verified byte-for-byte. AC 4 end-to-end at shell-invocation.
requirements.scope_notes: Core drift-class. Shared-source dedup + per-entry hash compare means any cross-entry contentHash inconsistency between siblings on the same sourcePath surfaces as a mismatch against at least one sibling's expected hash (story file line 54).

requirements.title: Removal drift (docs side) — INVARIANTS.md anchor removed without manifest removal → reports removed-from-docs-only, exits non-zero
requirements.substrate_verification: End-to-end via iter-8 Task-5 docs-side orphan-anchor smoke (append `INV-fake-orphan` anchor → exit 1 + `removed-from-docs-only`; revert; byte-identical restore) per story file line 143. Anchor-walker enumerates INVARIANTS.md anchors via `/^-\s+\*\*\`(INV-[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)+)\`\*\*/gm` regex (story file lines 52, 127). Manifest-row-without-anchor branch is the symmetric case. All 10 production anchors have matching manifest rows post-Task-2; AC-5 branch verified by ephemeral injection in the smoke.
requirements.scope_notes: Story 1.9 Task 2 closed the pre-existing `INV-ralph-halt-path-resolution` docs-only drift by adding the 10th manifest entry (story file lines 57–71). Baseline now clean → AC 5 branch not actively triggered.

requirements.title: CLI exit-code contract for CI — non-zero reliably fails the workflow; drift report renders in CI logs
requirements.substrate_verification: CLI contract pinned at check.ts: 0 on `status === 'clean'`, 1 on `status === 'drift'` (story file lines 81, 144). Structured JSON DriftReport printed to stderr on drift (exercised via AC 4 smoke). `pnpm keel-invariants:check` entry point wired at repo-root package.json.
requirements.scope_notes: Story 1.9 scope carve-out (AC 6 line 41, 126): CI workflow wiring itself lands with Epic 13 (F/E pipeline story). Story 1.9 delivers CLI + exit-code contract; Epic 13 wires the `.github/workflows/*.yml` step that invokes it.
requirements.level_notes: CLI contract (smoke-verified via AC 4 path); workflow-wiring deferred

requirements.title: Performance NFR — <2 second wall-clock when run on baseline repo
requirements.substrate_verification: Task 5 performance smoke: `time pnpm keel-invariants:check` → 0.77s wall-clock — well under AC 7's 2s budget; >2x headroom (story file lines 96, 145). Dedup of shared-source reads + `Promise.all` parallelism is load-bearing for the <500ms internal target.
requirements.scope_notes: Baseline substrate = 8 distinct source files (10 invariants dedupe to 8). sha256 on files this size (largest ~8KB) <1ms each; file-read IO dominates; cold <100ms expected, warm <20ms. 2s budget leaves ~20x headroom for growth to ~200 invariants.

recommendations.action: Defer AC-1 / AC-2 / AC-3 / AC-4 / AC-5 unit-test coverage to Story 1.16 (test-runner landing); Task 5 shell-invocation smoke tests already cover AC-1 + AC-2 + AC-4 + AC-5 + AC-7 end-to-end. AC-3 remains a small pure function branch that CR adversarial pass backstops at 1.9.

Defer AC-6 GitHub Actions workflow wiring to Epic 13 (F/E pipeline story) per spec scope carve-out. Story 1.9 ships the CLI + exit-code contract; Epic 13 wires the `.github/workflows/*.yml` invocation step.
AC-7 <2s performance budget met with >2x headroom (0.77s wall-clock). No follow-up needed unless substrate grows beyond ~200 invariants.
Run /bmad-code-review (args: "2") next — adversarial triage is the agreed backstop for AC-3 structural + AC-6 CLI-contract-only branches per § Testing Standards + Dev Agent Record AC coverage matrix.
